run_note: Light re-run after a source-provenance edit to topic 1.1 only: supporting reference [3] swapped from machinelearningmastery.com to Real Python 'Jupyter Notebook: An Introduction' (realpython.com/jupyter-notebook-introduction/), and one GPU citation moved [3]->[1]. All corpora, readings, quizzes, and the three diagrams (1.2/1.3/1.4) are unchanged from the prior PASS. All five original checks plus diagram coherence re-verified; the swap affects source provenance only and introduces no coherence drift.

terminology_consistency.summary: No synonym drift across the four topics. The 1.1 source swap changed only a supporting URL and a citation marker; it introduced no new term and altered no existing term. All established vocabulary and the diagram labels remain consistent.
evidence: The 1.1 corpus prose is textually unchanged by the swap: 'interpreter', 'interactive mode / REPL', 'Google Colab', 'hosted Jupyter notebook', 'notebook', 'cells', 'run order', 'runtime', 'restart the runtime', 'Google Drive', 'print()', 'function', 'string' all keep their introducing names. The new [3] source (Real Python Jupyter intro) uses the same cell/kernel-as-runtime/restart-clears-state vocabulary, so no term is renamed at the source layer either.

'variable' / 'assignment operator (=)' introduced in 1.2 5.1; reused verbatim in 1.3 (== vs =) and 1.4 5.1; 1.2 diagram edge 'assignment (=) binds to' uses 'bind' only as a verb, not a competing noun.
'identifier' and 'snake_case' introduced in 1.2 5.3/5.5 match the 1.2 diagram Name node label exactly.
'int, float, str, bool' spelled identically across 1.2 prose, the 1.2 TYPE node, and the 1.4 conversion-map nodes.
'operator precedence' / 'binds first' / 'binds last' in the 1.3 diagram match the 1.3 corpus ladder language.
'truthiness' and falsy '0'/'' -> False in the 1.4 diagram match 1.3 5.6 and 1.4 5.3; bool() still defers to '1.3 truthiness rules'.

prerequisites_respected.summary: Prerequisite chain is strictly increasing and matches sequence.index.json positions. The 1.1 source swap does not touch prerequisites or introduce any forward reference.
evidence: 1.1 prerequisites [] (position 1); 1.2 ['1.1'] (pos 2); 1.3 ['1.1','1.2'] (pos 3); 1.4 ['1.1','1.2','1.3'] (pos 4) — all strictly earlier. Unchanged by the swap.
The Real Python Jupyter intro is a supporting reference for 1.1's own scope (cells/runtime/print-era environment); it reaches neither forward nor backward across topics.
No diagram reaches forward; deferrals still point to later courses, not unseen earlier topics.

learning_arc.summary: Union of the four topics still fully delivers the week-1 narrative seed with no gaps and no redundancy. The 1.1 source swap changes provenance only; the environment/print() facet it supports is unchanged.
learning_arc.narrative_seed: Colab & print(); variables, identifiers, types; arithmetic/comparison/logical operators & precedence; type conversion; f-strings; type hints & match-case (intro)

learning_arc.notes: Arc unchanged from prior pass; source swap is confined to 1.1's supporting-reference provenance.

no_duplicated_core_concepts.summary: No pair of topics substantively re-teaches the same Core Concept. Shared concepts are introduced once and explicitly reused; the swap adds no new instructional content.
no_duplicated_core_concepts.pairwise_max_similarity: below 0.6 for all six pairs; highest overlap is 1.2<->1.3 on the bool/truthiness thread, a deliberate hand-off.
evidence: bool has three distinct roles (value-type in 1.2, truthiness/logical use in 1.3, conversion target in 1.4) — no re-teach.
Four types drawn as a single generic TYPE node in 1.2 vs four concrete conversion nodes in 1.4 — different purpose.
type() taught once in 1.2; PEP 8 snake_case taught in 1.2, extended (not repeated) in 1.4.
The new 1.1 reference (Real Python Jupyter intro) covers cells/runtime/restart, which is 1.1's own scope; it does not duplicate any downstream topic's Core Concepts.

lab_activity_alignment.summary: Every lab in the week-1 seed (P1.1-P1.7) is supported by at least one topic's hands-on artifact. The 1.1 source swap does not change lab alignment.
lab_activity_alignment.lab_seed: Labs P1.1-P1.7: Colab & print(); variables & types; arithmetic playground; assignment variants; booleans & precedence; conversion & f-strings; type hints & match-case.

diagram_coherence.summary: The three diagrams (1.2/1.3/1.4) are unchanged this run and continue to share one consistent visual grammar, remain corpus-faithful, and stay embedded inline. 1.1 correctly has no diagram, and the 1.1 source swap has no bearing on any diagram.

palette_consistency.shared_classdefs: All three .mmd files carry byte-identical classDefs: start fill:#a5d8ff stroke:#4a9eed; auto fill:#d0bfff stroke:#8b5cf6; done fill:#b2f2bb stroke:#22c55e. Same theme:base and themeVariables, same htmlLabels/curve:basis conventions. Unchanged.
palette_consistency.role_semantics: Blue 'start' = entry node, purple 'auto' = intermediate, green 'done' = terminal, applied consistently in every diagram. No inversion.

faithfulness: 1.2 binding mental-model faithful to corpus 'Mental model' + 5.8 dynamic typing.
1.3 operator-precedence ladder order matches the corpus precedence table (5.2) and Section 3 objectives.
1.4 type-conversion map arrows match corpus 5.3 behaviors; no spurious or missing edge.
embedding: 1.2/1.3/1.4 readings each embed their mermaid block inline with a lead-in sentence (project's live-mermaid convention); .mmd source and embedded block consistent. Unchanged.
diagram_coherence.standard_profile_check: 1.1 (standard, 7 min) has no diagram.mmd/.png and no diagram embed — correct, and unaffected by the source swap.
cross_topic_consistency: bool/truthiness drawn as a logic terminus in 1.3 and a conversion-target type in 1.4 — two distinct roles, labeled distinctly, no conflict.
Four value types: abstract single TYPE node in 1.2 vs four concrete nodes in 1.4 — complementary, no contradiction.
Assignment '=' appears only as a 1.2 diagram edge label; not re-drawn elsewhere.

source_swap_verification.scope: topic 1.1 supporting reference [3] only, plus one GPU-note citation marker moved [3]->[1]

topic_1_1_references.notes: Official Colab docs — hosted Jupyter, no setup, cells/runtime, GPU. Now carries the GPU citation moved from [3].
First-party QuantEcon Colab getting-started notebook — Python environment setup, cell run order.

Real Python 'Jupyter Notebook: An Introduction' (preferred trusted source). Cells, kernel/runtime, restart-clears-state, reading output; Colab as hosted Jupyter. Replaces the prior off-preferred-list machinelearningmastery.com blog.

source_swap_verification.citation_integrity: The 1.1 corpus still cites [1][2][3] coherently: [1] supports hosted-Jupyter/no-setup/cells/runtime-memory/GPU/save; [2] supports run order; [3] supports runtime-remembers/restart-clears-state (all covered by the Real Python Jupyter intro). The GPU note now correctly cites [1] (official Colab). No dangling or orphaned marker.
source_swap_verification.cross_topic_notes: 1.2 and 1.3 cite Real Python + GeeksforGeeks; 1.4 cites Real Python + PEPs. Adding Real Python's Jupyter intro to 1.1 aligns 1.1 with the module's already-established Real-Python-as-trusted-source preference. Each topic cites a DISTINCT Real Python article (jupyter-notebook-introduction / python-variables / python-operators-expressions / python-f-strings + python-string-formatting), so there is no duplicate-source or conflicting-source-of-truth issue across topics.
source_swap_verification.coherence_impact: none — provenance-only change; no learner-facing text, terminology, ordering, arc, overlap, lab, or diagram effect.

additional_assessments.finding: All three deep topics carry equal expected_delivery_minutes (10) and the same reading band, but their source-corpus sizes diverge (1.2 ~3310w, 1.3 ~5320w, 1.4 ~2650w) per the documented HITL#2 profile-upgrade tension.
additional_assessments.coherence_impact: None at the learner-facing layer. Carried forward from prior pass unchanged. No action required.

additional_assessments.finding: Sum of expected_delivery_minutes = 7 + 10 + 10 + 10 = 37 of a 90-min week target; remaining budget is absorbed by the week-1 lab track (P1.1-P1.7) and assessment prep.
